title: ranunculus acris var. serotinus
intro: Ranunculus acris var. serotinus, also known as the late buttercup, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus acris var. serotinus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxic properties and potential skin irritation.
